meta.description: A comment or note on another item, without modifying it. Like the other payload-dimension built-ins (alias, reference, document, …), an annotation item lives under the annotation type-UUID container and associates with its target via payload.targetId — not as a child of the annotated item. Its author is item.createdBy and its timestamp is item.createdAt; threaded replies nest via payload.parentAnnotationId.

targetId.description: UUID of the item this annotation is about. The annotation item lives under the annotation type-UUID container and associates with its target via this field (the dominant payload-dimension pattern, like alias/reference/document). Query all annotations on item X with: SELECT * FROM obj_<annotation-type> WHERE target_id = X.

body.description: The annotation text. Also mirrored to item.value for display/search.

parentAnnotationId.description: For a threaded reply, the UUID of the parent annotation; null for a top-level annotation. Threading lives here (not on item.parentId, which is always the annotation type-UUID container). Replies still carry the same targetId as the thread they belong to.

sqlSchema: -- Built-in type: adapter handles storage natively.
